data.title: Class Action Filed Against Nextracker Inc. (NXT) Seeking Recovery for Investors - Contact Levi & Korsinsky
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 20, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Nextracker Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/nextracker-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=131125&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

<img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/api/iframe.ashx?releaseid=988964&url=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fembed%2fks5blFZc6H4" width="0"/></div></div></div><p><strong>THE LAWSUIT:</strong> A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Nextracker Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between February 1, 2024 and August 1, 2024.</p><p><strong>CASE DETAILS:</strong> The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (a) the impact of project delays on Nextracker's business, financial results, and prospects was far more severe than represented to investors; (b) permitting and interconnection delays had materially impaired Nextracker's ability to convert backlog into revenue at historical conversion rates; (c) Nextracker had been unable to offset the negative impact from project delays through increased client demand and the purported ability to pull forward its other projects in the manner represented by defendants; (d) Nextracker did not possess the competitive advantages which purportedly shielded it from industry-wide headwinds or the ability to effectively offset the adverse effects of project delays as claimed by defendants; and (e) as a result of (a)-(d) above, defendants lacked a reasonable basis for their positive statements about Nextracker's business, financial results, and prospects.</p><p><strong>WHAT'S NEXT?</strong> If you suffered a loss in Nextracker stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to <a href="https://pr.report/7gp2" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/pslra-1/nextracker-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=131125&wire=1</a> to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. <strong>There is no cost or obligation to participate.</strong></p><p><strong>WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY:</strong> Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.</p><p><strong>CONTACT:</strong><br/>Levi & Korsinsky, LLP<br/>Joseph E. Levi, Esq.<br/>Ed Korsinsky, Esq.<br/>33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor<br/>New York, NY 10004<br/><a href="mailto:jlevi@levikorsinsky.com" rel="nofollow">jlevi@levikorsinsky.com</a><br/>Tel: (212) 363-7500<br/>Fax: (212) 363-7171<br/><a href="https://pr.report/7gp3" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/</a></p><p><strong>SOURCE:</strong> Levi & Korsinsky, LLP</p><br/><br/>View the original <a href="https://www.accessnewswire.com/newsroom/en/business-and-professional-services/class-action-filed-against-nextracker-inc-nxt-seeking-recovery-fo-988964">press release</a> on ACCESS Newswire<br/><br/><img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/img.ashx?id=988964" width="0"/></body></html>

data.title: China 10-Year Yield Hits 1-Month High
data.description:
China’s 10-year government bond yield climbed to around 1.74%, extending gains from the previous session and hitting a one-month high, fueled by ongoing optimism over a potential trade deal between the US and China.

US President Donald Trump's remarks suggesting that a new trade deal with China was possible have signaled a more positive outlook for US-China relations.

Earlier this week, Trump also stated that Chinese President Xi Jinping would visit the US, although no specific timeline was provided.

A potential meeting between the two leaders is seen as critical for easing or delaying existing trade tariffs, particularly as tensions between the US and China remain high due to longstanding disagreements, including trade practices and tariffs.

On the monetary front, the PBOC chose to keep its key lending rates unchanged for the fourth consecutive month in February 2025, with the one-year loan prime rate holding steady at 3.1%, while the five-year loan prime rate remained at 3.6%.

data.title: LG Electronics rebrands ultra-premium built-in kitchen appliance line
data.description: South Korean tech giant LG Electronics Inc. announced on Thursday that it has rebranded its ultra-premium built-in kitchen appliance brand, Signature Kitchen Suite, as SKS. SKS, launched in 2016, has gained recognition in top-tier premium markets such as North America and Europe, establishing itself as a leading built-in appliance brand backed by cutting-edge technology and consumer trust. While maintaining its brand philosophy of “True to Food,” LG Electronics aims to accelerate its expansion in the global built-in appliance market with the more intuitive SKS brand name, while solidifying its leadership in the sector. According to market research firm Euromonitor, the global built-in appliance market is expected to reach $64.5 billion this year. The United States, in particular, is the largest premium home appliance market and a representative builder’s market where architects and interior designers are the primary customers, unlike other countries where furniture companies dominate. B2B sales in the U.S. home appliance sector account for approximately 20 percent, with industry leaders such as General Electric Co. and Whirlpool Corp. boasting a strong presence in the B2B built-in appliance market for nearly a century. LG Electronics plans to unveil its expanded SKS lineup at KBIS 2025, the largest kitchen and bath trade show in the United States taking place from February 25 to 27 in Las Vegas. At the exhibition, the company will introduce a new island system concept, featuring hidden induction technology and an integrated hood for the first time. It will also showcase its new 36-inch Cook-zone-free Induction Pro Range, which incorporates an all-free induction system that automatically detects the size and position of cookware placed anywhere on the surface. The appliance also includes a built-in oven, allowing users to prepare multiple dishes at the same time. “With our newly rebranded ultra-premium built-in appliance brand SKS, we will continue to enhance our competitiveness in the built-in market, which is a key pillar of our B2B business,” said Lyu Jae-cheol, president of the LG Home Appliance Solution Company.

data.title: LGEs aims to shake market with dry electrode technology
data.description: LG Energy Solution Ltd. (LGES) unveiled its ambition to reshape the lithium iron phosphate (LFP) battery market with its breakthrough dry electrode technology. Speaking at the Battery Japan 2025 exhibit in Tokyo on Thursday, LGES Chief Technology Officer (CTO) Kim Je-young expressed confidence in the company’s dry electrode technology. “Implementing LFP batteries with the dry electrode process will allow us to reduce costs while significantly increasing energy density,” Kim explained. “We are also building a robust patent portfolio, with over 400 patents in the dry process.” The dry electrode technology replaces the traditional wet process used to produce battery electrodes, including both the anode and cathode. Instead of using liquid slurry, it employs solid powder and eliminates the need for drying solvents, enhancing production speed and efficiency. The technology also reduces the need for solvent drying and recovery equipment, leading to lower investment in machinery and smaller manufacturing space. The dry process also enables higher battery density, which can significantly extend driving range per charge. Kim revealed the company’s plans to secure mass production capabilities at the Ochang plant’s pilot line in 2025, with full-scale production of products incorporating the dry electrode process set to begin by 2028. The company believes that, by securing a competitive edge with this technology in the LFP battery market - currently dominated by Chinese battery companies - it can reshape the market by delivering both cost savings and improved performance. Kim also outlined the company’s vision for next-generation batteries, expected to drive future growth for LG Energy Solution. “We are accelerating the development of all-solid-state batteries using proprietary materials technology, with mass production targeted before 2030,” he said. The company is also working on lithium-sulfur batteries, which offer high energy density and cost competitiveness, for future aviation technologies such as urban air mobility (UAM) and high-altitude platform stations (HAPS). Kim emphasized the importance of securing patents to sustain continuous innovation even further. “LG Energy Solution holds a total of 37,874 registered patents and 68,158 filed patents across various fields, including materials, future technologies, processes, and battery management systems (BMS). We aim to create a virtuous cycle that strengthens our R&D competitiveness and drives technological product innovation.: Battery Japan event is the world’s largest annual exhibition for secondary batteries, showcasing cutting-edge technologies in battery materials, components, and manufacturing equipment. The event, which opened on Wednesday, will run through Friday in Tokyo.

data.title: Australia 10-Year Bond Yield Holds Steady
data.description:
Australia’s 10-year government bond yield steadied around 4.57% but remained near its highest level in over a month, as investors assessed fresh economic data.

Flash PMI figures indicated continued expansion in Australia’s business activity in February, supported by growth in both manufacturing and services sectors.

Meanwhile, earlier data showed that the country’s labor market remained strong in January, complicating the case for further interest rate cuts.

The Reserve Bank of Australia reduced its cash rate by 25bps to 4.10% on Tuesday but cautioned that further easing was not guaranteed due to persistent inflation risks.

Currently, markets see only a small chance of a rate cut in April, with expectations rising to 68% for a potential move in May.

data.title: Samsung Life speeds up Samsung Fire integration
data.description: Samsung Life Insurance Co. is accelerating efforts to integrate Samsung Fire & Marine Insurance Co. into its subsidiary amid a regulatory review. During a conference call on Thursday after its 2024 earnings announcement, Samsung Life emphasized that the integration would not affect its overall business operations, including profitability and capital ratios. The company also confirmed it is not considering acquiring additional shares in Samsung Fire, where it currently holds a 14.9 percent stake. Industry analysts believe that integrating Samsung Fire would benefit Samsung Life in enhancing its corporate value. In January, Samsung Fire announced a value-up initiative, which includes a share buyback and cancellation. If the buyback proceeds, Samsung Life’s stake could exceed the 15 percent regulatory threshold this year, requiring either a share sale or full subsidiary integration. Samsung Life, in the meantime, noted on Thursday that it has no plans to sell its 8.4 percent stake in Samsung Electronics Co. At the end of last year, Samsung Life’s Korean Insurance Capital Standard (K-ICS) ratio stood at 180 percent, down 39 percentage points from 219 percent at the end of 2023, due to a drop in the value of its Samsung Electronics holdings. Samsung Life reported a consolidated net income of 2.11 trillion won ($1.47 billion) last year and announced a record-high dividend of 4,500 won per share.

data.title: HD Hyundai’s hydrogen engine passes extreme cold test
data.description: HD Hyundai Infracore has successfully tested its hydrogen engine in extreme cold conditions, demonstrating its reliability at temperatures as low as -20°C. The company announced on Thursday that its 11-liter HX12 hydrogen engine passed a series of rigorous winter tests, including cold-start capability, stable fuel supply, hill climbing and braking under load, and refueling safety. The tests were conducted from February 4th to 14th, 2025, in Daegwallyeong, Gangwon Province, using a Tata Daewoo Mobility truck. HD Hyundai Infracore plans to begin mass production of hydrogen engines for trucks in early 2026, supplying them to Tata Daewoo Mobility. The first commercial hydrogen engine trucks are expected to launch in the first half of 2027. To ensure final performance validation, HD Hyundai Infracore will conduct additional heat endurance tests, hill-climbing capability assessments, 55°C high-temperature lab tests, and a 100,000 km durability drive before the official launch.

data.title: South Korea, Netherlands Strengthen Industrial Cooperation Amid Global Uncertainties
data.description:
South Korea's Trade Minister Dukgeun Ahn met Dutch Economic Affairs Minister Dirk Beljaarts in Seoul to enhance industrial cooperation between the two nations, the Ministry for Trade, Industry and Energy said Thursday.

Discussions covered economic security, supply chains, and semiconductor cooperation, with both sides emphasizing the importance of intergovernmental coordination. Ahn highlighted ongoing joint research and development in the ICT and bio sectors, expressing hopes for expanded technology partnerships through the bilateral joint innovation committee, it said.

The Netherlands, South Korea's second-largest EU trading partner and key investor, plays a crucial role in semiconductor and battery collaborations.

data.title: Pakistan's Lucky Cement Ltd Announces Five-For-One Stock Split Subject To Approval By Shareholders - Statement
data.description: • PAKISTAN'S LUCKY CEMENT LTD ANNOUNCES FIVE-FOR-ONE STOCK SPLIT SUBJECT TO APPROVAL BY SHAREHOLDERS - STATEMENT

data.title: Fulcrum Lithium Completes First Phase Drilling at Nevada Project
data.description:
Fulcrum Lithium completed its first phase drilling campaign at the Alkali Flats project in Nevada, which will inform plans for targeting other prospective areas around the Alkali Flats basin, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The drilling program was designed to test the Siebert Formation, a regional claystone host for lithium deposits, where initial surface sampling results showed lithium concentrations of up to 797 parts per million, the filing said.

The second phase of drilling at Alkali Flats is set to start in the second quarter, according to the filing.

data.title: Renaissance Asia Silk Road Resolves Long-Standing Winding-Up Petition
data.description:
A winding-up petition filed against Renaissance Asia Silk Road Group in December 2014 has been withdrawn, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The company has received a court note from the Hong Kong High Court on Wednesday, confirming the withdrawal.

The petition was filed in December 2014 by Victor Arise for an allegedly unpaid debt of about HK$3 million.

The company's share prices were up by 72% in recent trading.

data.title: HSBC Launches $2 Billion Share Buyback Program
data.description:
HSBC Holdings commenced a share buyback program with a maximum consideration of $2 billion, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The repurchases made in London will be considered on-exchange transactions, while the buybacks in Hong Kong will be off-market.

The purpose of the share buyback is to reduce the number of outstanding ordinary shares. These shares will eventually be canceled.

data.title: Megachem Swings to Profit in H2 2024
data.description:
Megachem booked a net attributable profit to equity holders of SG$5.9 million for the second half of 2024, rebounding from an attributable loss of SG$6.7 million a year earlier.

The chemicals company's earnings per share came in at SG$0.0443, compared with a loss per share of SG$0.0504 a year prior, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Revenue was up 2.2% year over year to SG$63.7 million from SG$62.3 million, mainly due to strong sales across North Asia and ASEAN markets.

The directors recommended a final dividend of SG$0.005 per share for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024.

data.title: Jefferies sees hospital stocks as pockets of strong earnings visibility in uncertain market
data.description:
Sentiment around hospital stocks has been on the rise, fuelled by aggressive capacity expansion as players tap into India's underpenetrated healthcare market. Brokerage firm Jefferies believes the sector offers strong earnings visibility in an uncertain market, making it a compelling investment opportunity.

Jefferies advises investors to view any weakness in hospital stocks as a buying opportunity, given the sector’s resilient growth. The brokerage favours hospitals with significant brownfield bed expansion plans in the coming years, ranking Max Healthcare as its top pick, followed by Fortis Healthcare, Apollo Hospitals, and Medanta operator Global Health.

While shares of Max Health, Apollo Hospitals and Fortis Healthcare have slumped 10-14 percent year-to-date, Global Health emerged as an outlier, gaining 10 percent in the same period.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live action

Meanwhile, Adani Group has announced its entry into the healthcare sector, unveiling plans to establish Mumbai’s largest private hospital on a seven-acre plot in Kandivli. The 1,000-bed facility will include a medical college. The Adani Group is set to invest Rs 6,000 crore to develop two 1,000-bed multi-specialty hospitals and medical colleges in Mumbai and Ahmedabad, in collaboration with US-based Mayo Clinic. However, Jefferies does not expect Adani's foray to significantly alter the competitive landscape of the sector.

Hospital stocks have faced headwinds due to calls for price standardisation and increased free bed allocations, leading to a sharp correction over the past two years. However, Jefferies notes that concerns have since eased, as investors realised that these regulatory risks had minimal impact on hospital operators.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: PBoC Pumps Liquidity to Ease Cash Shortage
data.description:
The People's Bank of China (PBoC) ramped up short-term cash injections on Friday to ease a liquidity squeeze that has unsettled the bond market.

The central bank injected a net CNY 84 billion through daily open market operations, marking the largest single-day infusion in February, according to Bloomberg News.

This move comes as the PBoC has been draining liquidity in most sessions, allowing repo rates to rise in an effort to support the yuan amid economic headwinds.

However, the tightening has pressured the bond market, pushing the 10-year benchmark yield to its highest level since December.

In its latest quarterly monetary policy report, the PBoC signaled that it would adjust and optimize policy size and pace based on domestic and global economic conditions.

data.title: Ringgit May Trade in 4.40-4.45 Range Versus Dollar Next Week — Market Talk
data.description: The Malaysian ringgit is expected to trade between 4.40 and 4.45 against the dollar next week, remaining in rangebound trading amid key global risks, Kenanga economists say in a note. Key near-term focuses include U.S. January core PCE inflation data due next week, potential U.S.-China trade negotiations, developments in the Russia-Ukraine war, and the trajectory of U.S. isolationist policies toward Europe, they note. Technically, the pair's outlook remains neutral and could hover around 4.433, they reckon. Kenanga pegs USD/MYR's short-term resistance at 4.437 and support at 4.410. USD/MYR is 0.3% lower at 4.4175. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: India's Sensex Opens 0.2% Lower at 75612.61
Ultrapower Software's Profit Jumps 62%, Operating Income Rises 8% in 2024; Shares Up 9%
data.description:
Beijing Ultrapower Software's net profit attributable to shareholders in 2024 jumped 62% year on year to 1.44 billion yuan or 0.7379 yuan per share in 2024, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

The attributable profit a year earlier was 887.2 million yuan or 0.4550 yuan per share.

The Chinese information technology company generated a total operating income of 6.45 billion yuan, up 8.2% from 5.96 billion yuan in the previous year.

The company's shares soared more than 9% in recent trade.

data.title: China to Boost Credit Support for Private Enterprises
data.description:
China's financial regulator pledged to expand credit support for private enterprises, emphasizing the need for stable credit growth.

The National Financial Regulatory Administration said it is also prioritizing first-time loans, renewals and credit for small businesses.

Banks and insurers will be guided to improve financial services, ensuring fair market access for businesses of all ownership types, the regulator added.

data.title: Huadong Medicine Unit Gets China Nod to Market Diabetes Drug; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
China's National Medical Products Administration accepted Huadong Medicine's marketing application for its degludec insulin injection, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

The drug is indicated to treat adult type 2 diabetes.

The pharmaceutical company has invested 122.9 million yuan in the research and development of the drug, the filing said.

The company's shares rose more than 3% in recent trade.

data.title: SingLand, UOL, Kheng Leong JV Secures Chinese Development Project with 8.9 Billion Yuan Bid
data.description:
Singapore Land's (SGX: U06) indirect joint venture company, Qin Rui Jia Realty, and China Jinmao Holdings were awarded a tender to acquire and develop a government land in Hong Kou, Shanghai, for a total consideration of about 8.96 billion yuan.

Qin Rui Jia Realty is a joint venture between SingLand subsidiary SingLand China Holdings, UOL unit UOL Capital Investments and Kheng Leong subsidiary Peak Star, with the companies holding respective stakes of 30%, 40% and 30% in the JV, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The land parcel will be converted into a residential project. Following the award of the tender, Qin Rui Jia and China Jinmao will enter into a joint venture agreement to form a JV company on a 10:90 basis. The JV will complete the purchase and development of the site.

The land has a total area of approximately 19,319 square meters with a 70 year leasehold.

data.title: CATL, Volkswagen to Collaborate on EV Batteries Amid Intensifying Competition
data.description:
By Jiahui Huang

Chinese battery giant Contemporary Amperex Technology will collaborate with Volkswagen on electric vehicle batteries, new materials and other auto parts as the automaker seeks to adapt to the fast-evolving and competitive EV market.

The Chinese company, in a statement released on Friday, announced that it has signed a memorandum of understanding with Volkswagen to strengthen its partnership in EV battery research and development, new material applications and the development of auto parts.

Volkswagen stated that partnering with CATL will help provide better battery solutions for its customers, support its supply chain and better meet the growing needs of the EV market.

The move comes amid intensifying competition in the Chinese EV market, where multiple local automakers are launching cheaper, more advanced EVs at a much faster pace than foreign brands.

Volkswagen has also teamed up with Chinese EV maker XPeng to build over 20,000 charging stations across 420 cities in China.

CATL accounted for more than one-third of the global battery market in 2024, more than twice the volume of BYD, which ranked second, according to Seoul-based SNE Research.

CATL's Shenzhen-listed shares rose 0.7% to 272.02 yuan, or equivalent of $37.49.

Write to Jiahui Huang at jiahui.huang@wsj.com

data.title: Multistack International Settles Legal Dispute with Danfoss, Secures Nearly $2 Million Payout
data.description:
Multistack International has reached a settlement in its legal dispute with Danish company Danfoss, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Under the settlement, Danfoss will make an immediate cash payment to the company of $1.9 million and will provide a one-off credit of $100,000 to be used for the purchase of goods from Danfoss, the filing added.

Danfoss has appointed the company to provide product support and on-site repairs and service for Danfoss products for five years, among other agreements under the settlement, per the filing.

The settlement is expected to be completed around March 7.

data.title: HKEX Likely to Remain Supported by Rise of AI Sentiment — Market Talk
data.description: Hong Kong Exchanges & Clearing is likely to remain supported by the rise of AI, which has fueled investor sentiment towards tech stocks, supporting a surge in turnover volume, according to Citi Research analyst Michael Zhang in a note. This support should persist in the near term, and HKEX is a key beneficiary of the recovery in market sentiment toward Chinese stocks, Zhang says. The increasing number of dual-listings of mainland companies in Hong Kong could also support the recovery of the Hong Kong IPO market, Zhang adds. Citi upgrades its rating on HKEX to buy from sell, raising the target price to HK$370 from HK$275. Shares are up 5.4% at HK$345.20. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0340 GMT - Hong Kong Exchanges & Clearing is likely to remain supported by the rise of AI, which has fueled investor sentiment towards tech stocks, supporting a surge in turnover volume, according to Citi Research analyst Michael Zhang in a note. This support should persist in the near term, and HKEX is a key beneficiary of the recovery in market sentiment toward Chinese stocks, Zhang says. The increasing number of dual-listings of mainland companies in Hong Kong could also support the recovery of the Hong Kong IPO market, Zhang adds. Citi upgrades its rating on HKEX to buy from sell, raising the target price to HK$370 from HK$275. Shares are up 5.4% at HK$345.20. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0335 GMT - Genting Singapore's cost pressures could ease in 2026, with earnings supported by new attractions, luxury rooms and retail stores, say DBS Group Research analysts in a note. The opening of its new all-suite hotel in Singapore has been delayed to 3Q, while its S.E.A. Aquarium will close for 2.5 months in 1H, ahead of the Singapore Oceanarium relaunch in July, they write. New retail stores will begin operating in the coming months, they say. Higher initial costs to prepare for these openings could soften 1H earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization margins, they say. "However, we expect a sequential recovery as income from the new openings starts to flow in," they add. DBS maintains the stock's buy rating and target price of S$0.95. Shares are at S$0.76.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0332 GMT - Kossan Rubber Industries' earnings are expected to weaken sequentially in 1Q before improving in later quarters, Hong Leong IB analyst Chee Kok Siang says in a note. Chee cuts the glove maker's 2025-2026 earnings forecasts by 49% and 31%, respectively, given its 4Q 2024 profit shortfall. The glove maker could see 8.5%-10.5% weaker sales volume for its glove and clean-room segment in 1Q as most front-loading activities by U.S. customers has already occurred in 4Q of 2024, he says. The global medical glove demand-supply balance is now projected to stabilize in 2026 instead of 2025, Chee adds. Hong Leong cuts the stock's target price to MYR2.81 from MYR3.00 but keeps a buy rating. Shares are 1.0% higher at MYR1.97. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0329 GMT - Foreign fund inflows into Chinese equities have picked up over the past two weeks as DeepSeek draws investors' attention to the market, HSBC Global Research analysts write in a note. The Chinese startup has shown that the country has the ability to compete at the highest level in the artificial-intelligence space, they add. The valuation gap between China and emerging markets has been narrowing to 12.5% from 20.3%, they note. DeepSeek has also triggered a rotation in the Chinese market from upstream hardware to midstream software and services as well as downstream applications, the analysts say. They note that 120 A-share companies have integrated DeepSeek into their products or services. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0323 GMT - Advanced Info Service's earnings may benefit from Thailand's likely economic growth, Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Wasu Mattanapotchanart says in a research report. This should lead to increases in the Thai mobile phone operator's prepaid top-ups such as for higher data consumption, the analyst says. The brokerage now forecasts the company's 2025 core profit to grow 12%, thanks to drivers including spectrum cost savings and falling interest expenses. The company's fixed broadband revenue should also outperform its mobile revenue due to growth in its fixed broadband subscribers. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to THB312.00 from THB307.00 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 0.35% lower at THB282.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0321 GMT - NetEase's fundamentals remain intact despite its lower-than-expected 4Q mobile game revenue, HSBC analysts say in a research note. NetEase's strong deferred revenue, which implies a solid performance of legacy PC games and a successful launch of "Marvel Rivals," could outweigh weakness in its mobile games and support game growth in 2025, they say. HSBC lowers its 2025 revenue forecast slightly as NetEase focuses on lifting the profitability of its nongame segments, which could weigh on topline growth in coming quarters. However, it raises its 2025-2026 profit estimate by 3% due to the game maker's expected better cost-efficiency. HSBC raises NetEase's ADR target to $120.00 from $117.00 and keeps a buy call. Shares last ended at $101.42.(sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0257 GMT - Nexon's 2025-2026 earnings outlook appears positive, Daiwa Capital Markets analysts say in a research report as they maintain the stock's buy rating. The Japan-listed game developer has a solid game pipeline targeting different game genres and addressable markets as well as strong cash-generating franchise intellectual properties, the analysts note. Nexon could deliver relatively decent, sustainable growth in revenue and operating profit this year, thanks to drivers including new game launches in March. However, Daiwa cuts its 2025-2026 EPS forecasts for Nexon by 6.8%-7.5% to partly reflect the management's revised cost strategy for staff and marketing. It lowers the stock's target price to Y3,000 from Y3,300. Shares are 0.4% higher at Y2,029. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0231 GMT - Thai Union Group may gain from its business transformation in 2025, UOB Kay Hian's Kampon Akaravarinchai says in a research report as the brokerage maintains a buy rating on the stock. The company expects US$15 million costs savings and US$17 million operating-profit increase for subsidiary i-Tail Corp., the analyst notes. It also said that some of these benefits will be used to reinvest in transformation projects and in developing skills for employees, the analyst adds. The brokerage trims the stock's target price to THB15.00 from THB15.50, based on estimated five-year price-to-earnings mean of 13X. Shares closed 2.5% lower at THB11.70 on Thursday. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0231 GMT - While AI and data center demand offer long-term potential for Inari Amertron, geopolitical risks and trade tensions could weigh on growth, Kenanga IB analyst Cheow Ming Liang writes in a note. The World Semiconductor Trade Statistics expects the global semiconductor market to grow 11.2% in 2025, but uncertainties remain amid rising trade tensions, which could impact the industry's short-term outlook, he notes. Cheow revises Inari's earnings estimates for FY2025 and FY2026 lower by 21% and 23%, respectively, as the company's fiscal 1H earnings fell short of expectations. Kenanga downgrades Inari's rating to market perform from outperform, while cutting the target price to MYR2.80 from MYR3.27. Shares are 3.0% lower at MYR2.57.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0228 GMT - Genting Singapore's earnings outlook will likely be generally stable, Maybank IB's Yin Shao Yang says in a note. The 'Minion Land' attraction opened in February, while a super luxury all-suite hotel and the Singapore Oceanarium are expected to open in 3Q, the analyst says. Parts of the Forum complex will also open over the course of the year, Yin adds. The integrated resorts operator expects 1H margins to be suppressed due to preopening expenses, but 2H margins could normalize, Yin notes. In the long term, Genting Singapore expects the attractions to be Ebitda accretive. Maybank maintains the stock's buy rating and target price of S$1.01. Shares are at S$0.76. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0217 GMT - CIMB Group's Indonesia unit could see an improved net interest margin in 1Q thanks to deposit repricing benefits from Bank Indonesia's January rate cut, Hong Leong IB analyst Chan Jit Hoong says in a note. Loan growth is expected to remain steady but unlikely to accelerate meaningfully, he reckons. With strong buffers in place, asset-quality risks appear manageable, he adds. CIMB Niaga generally contributes about 20%-25% to CIMB Group's profit before tax, he notes. CIMB's position as a large-cap, index-heavy stock makes it attractive as investors may seek refuge from uncertainties around Trump 2.0, he says. Hong Leong maintains a buy rating on CIMB and keeps the target price at MYR9.20. Shares are 0.2% lower at MYR8.33. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0159 GMT - Singtel's new FY 2025 earnings guidance looks encouraging, the OCBC Investment Research team says in note. The telecom conglomerate has raised its FY 2025 EBIT growth forecast to high teens-to-low twenties from prior forecast of low double digits, the team says. Singtel is likely a key beneficiary of Singapore's drive for technology and innovation as highlighted in recent Singapore Budget, the team says. The government will allocate S$150 million for a new "Enterprise Compute Initiative," which Singtel could tap to expand its enterprise services and cloud-computing offerings. OCBC raises stock's fair value estimate to S$4.00 from S$3.85 and maintains a buy rating. Shares are 2.1% lower at S$3.30. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Santos Target Price Raised 0.6% to A$8.15/Share by UBS>STO.AU
Malaysian Pacific's Profit Rises in Fiscal Q2
data.description:
Malaysian Pacific Industries' attributable profit rose to 40 million ringgit in the fiscal second quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, from 32.2 million ringgit a year earlier.

Earnings per share went up to 0.200 ringgit from 0.1617 ringgit a year ago, according to a Wednesday Malaysian bourse filing.

Revenue edged up year over year to 530.5 million ringgit from 522.8 million ringgit, the integrated circuit manufacturer said.

data.title: Mayur Resources Completes Bookbuild for AU$100.1 Million CDI Placement; Shares Fall Past 11%
data.description:
Mayur Resource completed the bookbuild for its around AU$100.1 million institutional placement of around 357.5 million new Chess depository interests (CDI) at an offer price of AU$0.28 per CDI, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The firm will issue around 106.5 million CDIs, raising around AU$29.8 million in the first tranche.

It also proposes to issue around 251 million new CDIs to raise around AU$70.3 million, subject to shareholder approval.

The proceeds will fund the development of the first and second stages of the company's Central Lime project in Papua New Guinea.

The firm's shares fell past 11% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Kossan Rubber Could See Sequentially Weaker 1Q Earnings — Market Talk
data.description: Kossan Rubber Industries' earnings are expected to weaken sequentially in 1Q before improving in later quarters, Hong Leong IB analyst Chee Kok Siang says in a note. Chee cuts the glove maker's 2025-2026 earnings forecasts by 49% and 31%, respectively, given its 4Q 2024 profit shortfall. The glove maker could see 8.5%-10.5% weaker sales volume for its glove and clean-room segment in 1Q as most front-loading activities by U.S. customers has already occurred in 4Q of 2024, he says. The global medical glove demand-supply balance is now projected to stabilize in 2026 instead of 2025, Chee adds. Hong Leong cuts the stock's target price to MYR2.81 from MYR3.00 but keeps a buy rating. Shares are 1.0% higher at MYR1.97. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)
